summary.name: High-ROE Large-Cap Quality Basket
summary.author: Jens.
summary.description:
The strategy is a monthly-updated US equity selection model that seeks to capture a profitability/quality premium by systematically allocating to stocks with the highest trailing one year return on equity (ROE) within a liquid large-cap universe. summary.description_full:
The strategy is a monthly-updated US equity selection model that seeks to capture a profitability/quality premium by systematically allocating to stocks with the highest trailing one year return on equity (ROE) within a liquid large-cap universe. The opportunity targeted is the persistent cross-sectional tendency for highly profitable firms to outperform, while mitigating implementation frictions by restricting the universe to seasoned listings and higher liquidity names.

On each month start, the investable universe is filtered to US stocks with fundamental coverage, price available, IPO older than 6 months, market capitalization above USD 500 million, and a valid ROE measure, while also excluding any stock that has been selected within the prior 12 months to reduce turnover and repeat exposure. From the remaining set, the model removes the least liquid decile by dollar volume and then selects the top 2 stocks by ROE; the portfolio holds the cumulative set of all monthly selections from the past 12 months (up to 24 names). The portfolio is rebalanced monthly into equal weights across all currently held names using daily fundamental/price inputs; the primary risk control is broad diversification via the rolling 12-month basket and equal-weight sizing, with no explicit stop-loss, leverage, or sector/market hedging.

On each month start, the investable universe is filtered to US stocks with fundamental coverage, price available, IPO older than 6 months, market capitalization above USD 500 million, and a valid ROE measure, while also excluding any stock that has been selected within the prior 12 months to reduce turnover and repeat exposure.
